name: New Year's Sesame Seed Balls
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cup Light brown or white sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the flour in a large mixing bowl.
2. Dissolve the light brown or white sugar in boiling water.
3. Stir the sugar solution into the flour until the flour is all damp.
4. If the dough appears too dry, add more hot water and mix until smooth.
5. Pat and knead the dough in the bowl until soft and smooth.
6. Break off half the dough and roll it between your palms; if not smooth, wet hands and roll again.
7. Roll each piece into a ball about the size of a walnut and cover with a damp towel or plastic wrap.
8. Press an indentation into one dough ball using a deep cup shape.
9. Place 1 teaspoon of red bean paste inside and close the cup by sealing the edge well.
10. Roll the filled ball between your palms to make it perfectly round, then roll in sesame seeds until fully covered.
11. Heat oil in a wok over medium heat to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
12. Lower several balls into the hot oil at a time.
13. When the sesame seeds turn lightly brown, apply pressure with the back of a large ladle to each ball, pressing them against the bottom or side of the pan.
14. Continue pressing until each ball triples in size and the sesame seeds are golden brown (about 5 minutes).
15. Drain the sesame balls on paper towels and serve warm or at room temperature. Reheated balls can be warmed in a preheated oven at 450 degrees Fahrenheit for about 5 minutes.
